1.title: Open Baseplate Settlement- Evaluation Model Panel

content.text: System shall open the Baseplate Settlement (G5) — Evaluation Model panel when the user selects the G5 test from the Rules and Actions → Testing tab.
open the Baseplate Settlement (G5) evaluation model panel
I can review the evaluation logic for the G5 test.
the user is on Rules and Actions → Testing tab
the user selects the G5 test
the system shall open the Baseplate Settlement (G5) — Evaluation Model panel.
System shall display the panel title “Baseplate Settlement (G5) — Evaluation Model”.
I clearly understand which evaluation model is being viewed.
the evaluation model panel is opened
the system shall display the title “Baseplate Settlement (G5) — Evaluation Model.”
System shall display a Global scope indicator beside the evaluation model title.
I understand the scope of the evaluation model.
the system shall display a Global scope indicator beside the evaluation model title.
System shall display a description indicating the evaluation is derived from base elevation survey data (Z coordinates).
I understand how the evaluation is derived.
the system shall display a description indicating the evaluation is derived from base elevation survey data (Z coordinates).
System shall display the Measurement Basis section describing that base elevation values (Z_base,j) are measured at each upright base location within a rack row.
I understand how elevation values are collected.
the system shall describe that base elevation values (Z_base,j) are measured at each upright base location within a rack row.
System shall display the definition Z_base,j = measured elevation at base of upright j.
the system shall display Z_base,j = measured elevation at the base of upright j.
System shall display that reference elevation Z_ref is the mean base elevation of the rack row.
see how reference elevation is defined
I understand how settlement is calculated.
the system shall display that reference elevation Z_ref is the mean base elevation of the rack row.
System shall display the settlement expression S_j = Z_base,j − Z_ref.
I understand how settlement values are derived.
the system shall display S_j = Z_base,j − Z_ref.
System shall display that absolute settlement governs: S_j
understand that absolute settlement is evaluated
I can interpret the results correctly.
the system shall display that absolute settlement is evaluated for the rack row – S_j.
System shall display that the evaluation is computed across all uprights within the same rack row.
know that evaluation occurs across the rack run
the system shall display that evaluation is computed across all uprights within the same rack row.
System shall display a note indicating minimum two base elevation measurements are required.
I can perform valid settlement calculations.
the system shall indicate that minimum two base elevation measurements are required.
System shall display a recommendation stating all uprights within the rack row should be measured.
the system shall recommend measuring all uprights within the rack row.
System shall display the Instrument Support section.
I understand which tools can be used.
the system shall display supported and unsupported instruments.
System shall display Drone + Laser → Supported under instrument support.
see if Drone + Laser is supported
I know it can be used for measurement.
the Instrument Support section is displayed
the system shall display Drone + Laser → Supported
System shall display Total Station → Supported under instrument support.
see if Total Station is supported
I know it can be used for survey measurement.
the Instrument Support section is displayed
the system shall display Total Station → Supported.
System shall display Plumb Bob → Not Supported under instrument support.
know if Plumb Bob is supported
the Instrument Support section is displayed
the system shall display Plumb Bob → Not Supported.
System shall display the Calculation Logic section in read-only mode.
I understand how settlement is computed.
the system shall display the section in read-only mode.
System shall display the calculation steps (Read only) for collecting base elevations and computing settlement values.
3. Settlement per upright: s_j = Z_base,j- Z_ref
I understand the process used to compute settlement values.
the Calculation Logic section is visible
the system shall display the steps for collecting base elevations and computing settlement values :
3. Settlement per upright: s_j = Z_base,j- Z_ref
System shall display a note within the Calculation logic section indicating S_max = maximum absolute vertical settlement within rack row (mm)
see a note explaining the meaning of S_max in the calculation logic section
I clearly understand what parameter represents the maximum vertical settlement used in evaluation.
the evaluation model panel is open,
the Calculation Logic section is displayed,
the system shows a note indicating that S_max represents the maximum absolute vertical settlement within the rack row (mm).
System shall display the RAG Criteria section as locked (non-editable).
the system shall display the section as locked (non-editable).
System shall display the RAG criteria Green when S_max ≤ 2 mm.
know when results are classified as Green
the system shall classify the result as Green.
System shall display the RAG criteria Amber when 2 mm < S_max ≤ 4 mm.
know when results are classified as Amber
the system shall classify the result as Amber.
System shall display the RAG criteria Red when S_max > 4 mm.
know when results are classified as Red
the system shall classify the result as Red.
System shall display a note indicating limits are based on rack support tolerance and localized slab settlement behavior.
see the rationale behind the thresholds
I understand the engineering guidance used.
the RAG criteria section is displayed
the system shall display a note explaining limits are based on rack support tolerance and localized slab settlement behavior.
System shall display an Output Preview section in the evaluation panel.
I can review computed settlement outputs.
the system shall display an Output Preview section.
System shall display S_max (mm): Auto under the Output Preview section.
I understand the governing settlement magnitude.
the system shall display S_max (mm) automatically.
System shall display Governing Upright ID: Auto under the Output Preview section.
I know where the maximum settlement occurs.
the system shall display Governing Upright ID automatically.
System shall display Settlement Direction (Up/Down): Auto under the Output Preview section.
I understand whether the base moved upward or downward.
the system shall display Settlement Direction (Up/Down).
System shall display Reference Elevation (Z_ref): Auto under the Output Preview section.
I understand the baseline used for settlement calculations.
the system shall display Reference Elevation (Z_ref).
System shall display Rack Row ID: Auto under the Output Preview section.
I know which rack run the evaluation applies to.
the Output Preview section is displayed
the system shall display the Rack Row ID automatically.
System shall display RAG: Auto under the Output Preview section.
I understand the settlement evaluation outcome.
the system shall display the RAG classification automatically.
System shall allow the user to close the panel using the Close (X) icon in the panel header.
close the evaluation panel using the header icon
I can quickly exit the panel.
the user clicks the Close (X) icon
the system shall close the panel.
System shall provide a Close button to exit the evaluation panel.
close the evaluation panel using a button
I can exit the evaluation view.
the user clicks the Close button
the system shall close the panel.
System shall return the user to the Rules and Actions → Testing tab when the panel is closed.
return to the Testing tab after closing the panel
I can continue working in Rules and Actions.
the system shall return the user to Rules and Actions → Testing tab.
